# services.opensnitch

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.opensnitch`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.opensnitch.configFile` | `absolute path` | Path to JSON config file. See: <https://github.com/evilsocket/opensnitch/blob/master/daemon/data/default-config.json> If this option is set, it will override any configuration done in options.services.opensnitch.settings. |
| `services.opensnitch.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Opensnitch application firewall. |
| `services.opensnitch.package` | `package` | The opensnitch package to use. |
| `services.opensnitch.rules` | `open submodule of (JSON value)` | Declarative configuration of firewall rules. All rules will be stored in `/var/lib/opensnitch/rules` by default. Rules path can be configured with `settings.Rules.Path`. See [upstream documentation](https://github.com/evilsocket/opensnitch/wiki/Rules) for available options. |
| `services.opensnitch.settings` | `open submodule of (JSON value)` | opensnitchd configuration. Refer to [upstream documentation](https://github.com/evilsocket/opensnitch/wiki/Configurations) for details on supported values. |
| `services.opensnitch.settings.Audit.AudispSocketPath` | `absolute path` | Configure audit socket path. Used when `settings.ProcMonitorMethod` is set to `audit`. |
| `services.opensnitch.settings.Ebpf.ModulesPath` | `null or absolute path` | Configure eBPF modules path. Used when `settings.ProcMonitorMethod` is set to `ebpf`. |
| `services.opensnitch.settings.Firewall` | `one of "iptables", "nftables"` | Which firewall backend to use. `nftables` ruleset can be used for `iptables` firewall too, if `iptables` is built with nftables compatibility. |
| `services.opensnitch.settings.ProcMonitorMethod` | `one of "ebpf", "proc", "ftrace", "audit"` | Which process monitoring method to use. |
| `services.opensnitch.settings.Rules.Path` | `absolute path not in the Nix store` | Path to the directory where firewall rules can be found and will get stored by the NixOS module. |
| `services.opensnitch.upstreamDefaults` | `boolean` | Whether to base the config declared in {option}`services.opensnitch.settings` on the upstream example config (<https://github.com/evilsocket/opensnitch/blob/master/daemon/data/default-config.json>) Disable this if you want to declare your opensnitch config from scratch. |
